title: Fix Login Page Display and Internet Identity Verification Flow

requirements.text: Restore `frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` to a fully functional, visually complete login page with AstraVault branding: matte black (#0a0a0a) background, gold (#c9a84c / #d4af37) and military-green (#2d4a1e / #3a5c28) accents, shield/command iconography, Rajdhani/Inter fonts, and a single 'Login with Internet Identity' button wired to the `useInternetIdentity` hook's `login` function. Already-authenticated users (identity resolved and non-anonymous) must be automatically redirected to `/`. The page must be accessible at the `/login` route for all unauthenticated users.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to `/login` renders the AstraVault login page with matte black background and gold/green accents
The 'Login with Internet Identity' button is visible and triggers the Internet Identity login popup when clicked
An already-authenticated user visiting `/login` is automatically redirected to `/`
The page uses Rajdhani/Inter fonts consistent with the military theme
No blank screen, missing content, or JS errors appear on the login route

requirements.text: Ensure `frontend/src/main.tsx` configures the `InternetIdentityProvider` with the correct `identityProvider` URL for each environment: `http://rdmx6-jaaaa-aaaaa-aaadq-cai.localhost:4943` when `import.meta.env.DEV` is `true`, and `https://identity.ic0.app` for production. Remove any hardcoded, unreachable, or malformed identity provider URL that causes the Internet Identity iframe to hang on 'Securing Connection'. Use `import.meta.env.DEV` or `import.meta.env.VITE_DFX_NETWORK` to select the correct URL at build time.
acceptanceCriteria: In development mode (`import.meta.env.DEV === true`), `identityProvider` resolves to `http://rdmx6-jaaaa-aaaaa-aaadq-cai.localhost:4943`
In production mode, `identityProvider` resolves to `https://identity.ic0.app`
The Internet Identity popup opens successfully without hanging on 'Securing Connection'
No hardcoded or unreachable identity provider URL exists in `main.tsx`

requirements.text: Restore `frontend/src/hooks/useActorReady.ts` so that `isActorReady` is `true` only when all three conditions are strictly met in order: (a) `isInitializing` from `useInternetIdentity` is `false`, (b) the principal is non-anonymous (not equal to `Principal.anonymous()`), and (c) the actor instance returned by `useActor` is non-null. The hook must never flip `isActorReady` to `true` prematurely. Export `isActorReady` as a stable boolean.
acceptanceCriteria: `isActorReady` is `false` while `isInitializing` is `true`
`isActorReady` is `false` when the principal equals `Principal.anonymous()`
`isActorReady` is `false` when the actor instance is `null`
`isActorReady` flips to `true` only after all three conditions are simultaneously satisfied
No premature `true` value causes queries to fire before the actor is ready

requirements.text: Restore `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` so the `useGetProfile` query: (1) has its `enabled` flag strictly gated on `isActorReady === true`; (2) includes the authenticated principal string in the React Query cache key (e.g. `['profile', principalText]`) so it auto-invalidates when the principal changes; (3) treats a `null` / empty optional returned by the backend as 'profile not found' (new user flow), not as an error; (4) treats only a thrown exception as a genuine fetch failure (`isError`). All other query hooks in the file that call backend functions must also gate their `enabled` flag on `isActorReady`.
acceptanceCriteria: `useGetProfile` does not fire before `isActorReady` is `true`
The React Query cache key includes the principal string (e.g. `['profile', principalText]`)
A `null` response from `getProfile` sets `data` to `null` and does not set `isError` to `true`
A thrown exception from `getProfile` sets `isError` to `true`
All other backend-calling queries in `useQueries.ts` are gated on `isActorReady`

requirements.text: Restore `frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx` to the strict state machine: (1) While `isActorReady` is `false` OR the profile query `isLoading` is `true` → render a full-page military-themed loading spinner (no redirect, no children rendered). (2) If `isError` is `true` → render a full-page error state with a gold-accented 'Retry' button that calls `refetch()`. (3) If the user is definitively unauthenticated (not logged in AND actor initialization is complete) → redirect to `/login`. (4) If `data` is `null` (profile not found, new user) → render `<ProfileSetupModal>` without redirecting. (5) If profile data is present → render `<Outlet />` / children. The component must never redirect to `/login` during the loading phase.
acceptanceCriteria: A full-page military-themed loading spinner is shown while `isActorReady` is `false` or profile query is loading
No redirect to `/login` occurs while loading is in progress
A 'Retry' button with gold accent is shown when the profile query errors; clicking it calls `refetch()`
Unauthenticated users are redirected to `/login` only after actor initialization is confirmed complete
New users (null profile) see `<ProfileSetupModal>` instead of being redirected
Authenticated users with a profile see the protected page content (`<Outlet />`)

requirements.text: Restore `frontend/src/App.tsx` to the Version 4 routing structure with routes: `/login` (public, LoginPage), `/` (protected, DashboardPage), `/vault` (protected, VaultPage), `/settings` (protected, SettingsPage). The `AuthGuard` component must render only the full-page military-themed loading spinner while `isInitializing` is `true` and must never redirect to `/login` or render protected content until `isInitializing` has definitively settled to `false`.
acceptanceCriteria: Routes `/login`, `/`, `/vault`, and `/settings` are all defined and functional
While `isInitializing` is `true`, only the full-page loading spinner is rendered — no routing decision is made
After `isInitializing` settles to `false`, unauthenticated users are redirected to `/login`
Authenticated users can access `/`, `/vault`, and `/settings`
No blank screen or premature redirect occurs during the initialization phase

requirements.text: Restore `frontend/src/components/ProfileSetupModal.tsx` to the Version 4 design: a full-screen modal for new users to register a profile with fields for name, username, optional email, and vault password (SHA-256 hashed client-side before submission). On successful registration, invalidate the `['profile', principalText]` React Query cache key so `ProtectedRoute` immediately transitions from the modal to the dashboard. Style the modal with the military theme (matte black background, gold accents, military-green buttons).
acceptanceCriteria: New users (null profile) see the ProfileSetupModal after Internet Identity login
The modal collects name, username, optional email, and vault password
The vault password is SHA-256 hashed before being sent to the backend
On successful profile creation, the profile React Query cache is invalidated and the modal closes, revealing the dashboard
The modal is styled with matte black background, gold accents, and military-green buttons consistent with the military theme

requirements.text: Audit `backend/main.mo` — specifically the `getProfile` function — to confirm it returns `null` (empty optional `?UserProfile`) when no profile exists for the caller principal, and only traps or returns an error for genuine runtime failures. If it currently traps on a missing profile or returns an error variant for 'not found', fix it to return `null` instead. Do not change any other backend logic or data structures.
acceptanceCriteria: `getProfile` returns `null` (empty optional) when the caller principal has no registered profile
`getProfile` does not trap or throw for a missing profile
A genuine runtime error (e.g. storage failure) still results in a trap or error variant as appropriate
No other backend functions or data structures are modified

constraints: Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui
All Internet Identity authentication must use the existing useInternetIdentity hook — no third-party auth providers
Do not change any backend data structures or CRUD logic beyond fixing the getProfile null-return behavior
nonGoals: Adding new authentication methods beyond Internet Identity
Changing the visual theme beyond restoring the Version 4 military palette
Modifying sidebar links, vault notes, settings sections, or custom link manager behavior
Adding new pages or routes beyond the four existing ones
